tabs.title: Company Overview
tabs.content:
**TerraSync Logistics** is a 15-year-old supply chain management company operating a cloud-based platform that tracks shipments, manages warehouse inventory, and optimizes delivery routes for over 2,000 logistics partners worldwide.

The platform currently runs on aging infrastructure with a mix of AWS services and legacy on-premises systems. TerraSync is undertaking a modernization initiative to migrate remaining on-premises workloads to AWS, adopt serverless architectures where appropriate, and implement cost optimization strategies.

The company processes 3 million shipment tracking events daily, stores 10 years of historical shipment data (approximately 200 TB), and provides real-time visibility to supply chain stakeholders through web and mobile applications.

tabs.title: Current Environment
tabs.content:
**AWS Resources:**
- EC2-based web application tier (50 m5.large instances in Auto Scaling groups)
- Amazon RDS MySQL database (db.r5.8xlarge) for active shipment data
- Amazon S3 for document storage (shipping labels, POD images, invoices)
- On-premises data center with 30 TB of archived shipment data
- VPN connection between on-premises network and AWS VPC

**Application Characteristics:**
- Web application: 500,000 page views per day
- REST API: 2 million requests per day
- Batch processing: Nightly ETL jobs processing previous day's events
- Mobile API: Real-time shipment tracking updates
- Reporting: Ad-hoc queries on historical data (10 years)

**Pain Points:**
- High monthly costs: $85,000 for EC2 and RDS alone
- S3 storage costs growing 15% monthly due to document retention requirements
- Slow response times for historical data queries
- Idle compute resources during off-peak hours (nights and weekends)
- Maintenance overhead for EC2 fleet patching and scaling

tabs.title: Modernization Goals
tabs.content:
**Technical Goals:**
- Migrate 30 TB of archived data from on-premises to AWS
- Implement serverless architecture for infrequently accessed API endpoints
- Reduce S3 storage costs by implementing intelligent lifecycle policies
- Improve historical query performance using appropriate AWS analytics services
- Decommission on-premises data center (target: 6 months)
- Establish network connectivity between AWS VPCs in US, EU, and Asia regions

**Cost Optimization Goals:**
- Reduce overall AWS spending by 35% within 12 months
- Right-size EC2 instances based on actual utilization patterns
- Optimize S3 storage costs through lifecycle policies (estimated savings: $15,000/month)
- Evaluate Savings Plans and Reserved Instances for predictable workloads
- Implement automated start/stop schedules for non-production environments

**Architecture Goals:**
- Adopt API Gateway + Lambda for new API endpoints to reduce operational overhead
- Migrate nightly batch jobs to serverless processing (Lambda, Step Functions)
- Implement CloudFront for global content delivery and reduced origin load
- Establish hub-and-spoke network topology connecting regional VPCs
- Move from VPN to Direct Connect for consistent network performance
